body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

## agent_supports: yes

Pi 0.70.0 ships a manual `/compact` slash command (registered in dist/core/slash-commands.js as `name: "compact"`; the CHANGELOG references it directly â€” "Fixed `/compact` to reuse the session thinking level for compaction summaries"). It triggers a real LLM summarization round and, on completion, writes a single top-level `{"type":"compaction","summary":...,"firstKeptEntryId":...,"tokensBefore":...}` entry to the session via SessionManager.appendCompaction() â†’ _appendEntry() (dist/core/session-manager.js:617-621). Crucially the compact path calls _appendEntry on the CURRENT leaf â€” there is NO newSession()/createSessionId() in it, so compaction stays inside the SAME rollout file and the SAME session UUID. (Pi also supports automatic compaction on context-window pressure via CompactionSettings/CompactionEnabled, but that is NOT deterministically reproducible in a bounded recording; the manual `/compact` is the deterministic trigger this cell uses.)

Empirically confirmed by a standalone driver probe (2026-05-25, pi 0.70.0): send 'ok' â†’ wait_turn â†’ /compact â†’ send 'still-here'. ONE session file (`...02-34-02-757Z_019e5cfb...jsonl`, single id throughout) with 8 lines: session header, model_change, thinking_level_change, user, assistant/stop (priming turn), `{type:"compaction",summary}` at 02:34:43 (~8.3s after the priming stop â€” a real model round), then user + assistant/stop (post-compaction turn). No second file, no waiting, no supersession.

## daemon_capability: full; driver_capability: ready

The pi parser handles compaction explicitly (core/adapters/inbound/agents/pi/parser.go:55-60): `eventType == "compaction"` â†’ `ev.EventType = "assistant"`, i.e. active model work that promotes readyâ†’working (covered by TestParser_Compaction_IsActivityEvent and TestParser_Compaction_SetsLastEventToAssistant). Since the compaction line is separated from the priming ready by several seconds, the daemon emits it as its OWN workingâ†’ready arc on the same session â€” exactly the scenario's user-observable signal (state arc across the compaction, no intervening waiting, session continues). Pi Source = FilesUnderRoot watching ~/.pi/agent/sessions (agent.go), and the compaction entry lands in the existing rollout file, so there is no new session_id and no transcript-stitching dependency.

Why `partial`, not `yes`: irrlicht cannot LABEL the turn as a compaction. The parser collapses the compaction event into a generic assistant/working activity; the domain CompactionState field (session.go:16-18, 550) has no production writer and StringState's compaction branch is dead code, so the dashboard shows compaction as an ordinary working turn â€” indistinguishable from a normal long turn. The state ARC is fully observed (hence applicable: yes); the compaction-vs-normal-turn DISTINCTION is not (hence irrlicht_observes downgraded to partial). The spec asserts only the arc, which is the user-observable contract.
caveats: irrlicht cannot distinguish a compaction turn from a normal working turn: parser.go maps the `compaction` event to a generic assistant/working activity, and the domain CompactionState field is dead code (no production writer; StringState's compaction branch never fires). The dashboard shows compaction as an ordinary working turn. This is fine for the spec contract (state arc only); surfacing compaction in the UI would be a separate ticket â€” the reason irrlicht_observes is `partial` not `yes`.

Pi also supports AUTOMATIC compaction on context-window pressure (CompactionSettings/CompactionEnabled), but auto-compaction is not deterministically reproducible in a short bounded recording. This cell uses the MANUAL `/compact` slash command, which is deterministic and produces the same `{type:"compaction"}` entry on the same session.

DRIVER-SYNC: pi's `/compact` writes a `compaction` entry, NOT an assistant `stopReason==stop` message, so drive-pi-interactive.sh's turn-counter does not increment for it. The recipe deliberately omits a counted `wait_turn` after `/compact` (which would inflate EXPECTED_TURNS and force a driver timeout) and instead sleeps over the compaction round, then issues the post-compaction prompt with a trailing sleep to flush its turn before teardown. No driver extension is needed â€” only `send`/`slash`/`wait_turn`/`sleep`, all of which the pi driver already implements.

sources.note: `name: "compact"` is a registered slash command in pi 0.70.0 â€” primary evidence the manual /compact trigger ships.

Lines 617-621 appendCompaction(): writes a {type:"compaction",summary,firstKeptEntryId,tokensBefore,...} entry via _appendEntry on the CURRENT leaf â€” no newSession()/createSessionId(), so compaction stays inside the same rollout file / same session UUID (no boundary file, no supersession).

Lines 55-60: eventType=="compaction" â†’ EventType="assistant" (active model work, readyâ†’working). Confirmed by TestParser_Compaction_IsActivityEvent and TestParser_Compaction_SetsLastEventToAssistant. The compaction-vs-normal distinction is NOT surfaced â€” the event collapses into a generic assistant activity.

Lines 16-18, 550, 598-601: CompactionState constants + field + StringState branch exist but have NO production writer â€” dead code. The daemon cannot label a turn as compaction; hence irrlicht_observes is partial.

Live probe rollout (2026-05-25): one session id throughout; priming user+assistant/stop, then a {type:"compaction"} entry at 02:34:43 (~8.3s after priming stop), then post-compaction user+assistant/stop. No new file, no waiting.

context-compaction set to applicable: yes at confidence 0.80; pi supports manual /compact, the parser maps the compaction event to assistant(working) (no new session, no waiting). Spec asserts the lifecycle arc; the compaction label is not phase-expressible (CompactionState dead code) so it is documented in spec notes.
